code:
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import List

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: List[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of food‑related triples into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Build a graph of related entities for better sentence construction
    subject_to_clauses = {}
    subject_to_objects = {}

    for t in triples:
        s, p, o = t.subject, t.predicate, t.object
        if s not in subject_to_objects:
            subject_to_objects[s] = []
        subject_to_objects[s].append((p, o))

    # Build clauses with better sentence structure
    all_clauses = []

    for s, predicates in subject_to_objects.items():
        if len(predicates) == 1:
            p, o = predicates[0]
            clause = _phrase_predicate(s, p, o)
            all_clauses.append(clause)
        else:
            # Multiple predicates for same subject - combine them
            first_p, first_o = predicates[0]
            base_clause = _phrase_predicate(s, first_p, first_o)

            # Add additional info with relative clauses
            if len(predicates) > 1:
                additional_info = []
                for p, o in predicates[1:]:
                    additional_info.append(_phrase_predicate(s, p, o))

                if additional_info:
                    # Combine with relative clause structure
                    combined = base_clause + " which " + additional_info[0].replace(s, "it")
                    for info in additional_info[1:]:
                        combined += ", " + info.replace(s, "it")
                    all_clauses.append(combined)
                else:
                    all_clauses.append(base_clause)
            else:
                all_clauses.append(base_clause)

    # Assemble into one coherent sentence
    if not all_clauses:
        return ""
    elif len(all_clauses) == 1:
        return all_clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        # Connect clauses with appropriate conjunctions
        result = all_clauses[0]
        for clause in all_clauses[1:-1]:
            result += ", " + clause
        if len(all_clauses) > 1:
            result += ", and " + all_clauses[-1]
        return result + "."

def _phrase_predicate(s: str, p: str, o: str) -> str:
    """Convert a single triple into a natural language clause."""
    phrasing_map = {
        "country": f"{s} is from {o}",
        "ingredient": f"{s} contains {o}",
        "region": f"{s} originates from {o}",
        "division": f"{s} belongs to the division {o}",
        "family": f"{s} is in the family {o}",
        "order": f"{s} is classified under the order {o}",
        "course": f"{s} is served as a {o}",
        "dishVariation": f"{s} has variations such as {o}",
        "mainIngredient": f"The main ingredients of {s} are {o}",
        "leader": f"The leader of {s} is {o}",
        "alternativeName": f"{s} is also known as {o}",
        "creatorOfDish": f"{s} was created by {o}",
        "creator": f"{s} was created by {o}",
        "servingTemperature": f"{s} is served {o}",
        "served": f"{s} is served {o}",
        "carbohydrate": f"{s} contains {o} of carbohydrates",
        "fat": f"{s} contains {o} of fat",
        "protein": f"{s} contains {o} of protein",
        "year": f"{s} was introduced in {o}",
        "related": f"{s} is related to {o}",
        "similarDish": f"{s} is similar to {o}",
        "genus": f"{s} belongs to the genus {o}",
        "language": f"The language of {s} is {o}",
        "isPartOf": f"{s} is part of {o}",
        "currency": f"The currency of {s} is {o}",
        "demonym": f"The demonym for {s} is {o}",
        "capital": f"The capital of {s} is {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": f"The ethnic group of {s} is {o}",
        "foundationPlace": f"{s} was founded in {o}",
        "officialLanguage": f"The official language of {s} is {o}",
        "leaderTitle": f"The leader title of {s} is {o}",
        "servingSize": f"The serving size of {s} is {o}",
    }
    return phrasing_map.get(p, f"{s} {p} {o}")

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
